trans-unit.source: Spatial Queries
trans-unit.target: Spatial Queries

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: Manual selection
trans-unit.target: Manual selection

trans-unit.source: In the toolbar, select the ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_selection.png) `Select Features` tool.
trans-unit.target: In the toolbar, select the ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_selection.png) `Select Features` tool.

trans-unit.source: Select features individually by clicking on a feature.
trans-unit.target: Select features individually by clicking on a feature.

trans-unit.source: To select several features, you can press and hold <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> (<kbd>Cmd</kbd> on MacOS) and select one feature after the other.
trans-unit.target: To select several features, you can press and hold <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> (<kbd>Cmd</kbd> on MacOS) and select one feature after the other.

trans-unit.source: Selected features will be highlighted in bright yellow.
trans-unit.target: Selected features will be highlighted in bright yellow.

trans-unit.source: If you open the [attribute table](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html), the selected feature will appear in blue.
trans-unit.target: If you open the [attribute table](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html), the selected feature will appear in blue.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Manually select countries :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Manually select countries :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Select by Location
trans-unit.target: Select by Location

trans-unit.source: QGIS lets you select features based on their location using the tool `Select by Location`.
trans-unit.target: QGIS lets you select features based on their location using the tool `Select by Location`.

trans-unit.source: The tool uses spatial queries to select features. These look at the spatial relations between a set of features.
trans-unit.target: The tool uses spatial queries to select features. These look at the spatial relations between a set of features.

trans-unit.source: For example two features can intersect, or another feature is fully contained within another, or two features do not touch each other (disjoint).
trans-unit.target: For example two features can intersect, or another feature is fully contained within another, or two features do not touch each other (disjoint).

trans-unit.source: QGIS uses the following spatial relations: Intersect, Contain, Disjoint, Equal, Touch, Overlap, Are Within, Cross.
trans-unit.target: QGIS uses the following spatial relations: Intersect, Contain, Disjoint, Equal, Touch, Overlap, Are Within, Cross.

trans-unit.source: Check out the [QGIS documentation on spatial relations](https://docs.qgis.org/3.40/en/docs/user_manual/processing_algs/qgis/vectorselection.html#exploring-spatial-relations) to learn the differences between each spatial relation.
trans-unit.target: Check out the [QGIS documentation on spatial relations](https://docs.qgis.org/3.40/en/docs/user_manual/processing_algs/qgis/vectorselection.html#exploring-spatial-relations) to learn the differences between each spatial relation.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select all cities in China (`intersect`) :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select all cities in China (`intersect`) :open:
:::

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select all neighboring countries of China (`touch`) :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select all neighboring countries of China (`touch`) :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Export Selection
trans-unit.target: Export Selection

trans-unit.source: Once selected, you can further manipulate the features by, for example, exporting them to a new layer:
trans-unit.target: Once selected, you can further manipulate the features by, for example, exporting them to a new layer:

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer with the selected features → `Export` → `Save selected features as..`
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer with the selected features → `Export` → `Save selected features as..`
